translations.source: ድብቅ ቂጥኝ ምንም ምልክት የሌለው እና በአዎንታዊ የቂጥኝ ሴሮሎጂ ምርመራ ተለይቶ ይታወቃል። ትሪኮሞኒይስስ፡ ዋና ዋናዎቹ ምልክቶች ከሴት ብልት የሚወጣ ያልተለመደ ፈሳሽ ከሴት ብልት መቅላት፣ ማሳከክ እና የሚያሰቃይ ግንኙነት ይገኙበታል። የአባላዘር ሄርፒስ ሲምፕሌክስ ቫይረስ (ኤች ኤስ ቪ) ኤች ኤስ ቪ በአብዛኛው የሚከሰተው በውጫዊ የወሲብ አካላት እና በአፍ ላይ በአሰቃቂ ቁስሎች፣ ቫስኩሎች ወይም ቁስሎች ነው። የበሽታው ምልክቶች የሚታዩበት የዘር ብልት ኤች.ኤስ.ቪ በተደጋጋሚ የበሽታ ምልክቶች የሚከሰቱበት የዕድሜ ልክ በሽታ ነው። የሰው ቲ-ሊምፎትሮፒክ ቫይረስ ዓይነት 1 (ኤችቲኤልቪ-1) በአጠቃላይ ምልክት የሌለው፣ ሥር የሰደደ የ ኤችቲኤልቪ-1 ዓይነት ከባድ በሽታን ሊያስከትል ይችላል፣ ይህም የጎልማሳ ቲ-ሴል የሉኪሚያ/ ሊምፎማ( ኤቲኤል) እና ኤችቲኤልቪ-1-ተዛማጅ የአጥንት በሽታ ወይም ትሮፒካል ስፓስቲክ ፓራፓሬሲስ( ኤችኤኤም/ ቲኤስፒ) በመባል የሚታወቀው ቀስቃሽ የነርቭ ሥርዓት ሁኔታ ነው። ሕክምና፡ ውጤታማ ሕክምና በአሁኑ ጊዜ ለብዙ የአባላዘር በሽታዎች ይገኛል። ሶስት የባክቴሪያ የአባላዘር በሽታዎች (ክላሚዲያ፣ ጨብጥ እና ቂጥኝ) እና አንድ ጥገኛ የአባላዘር በሽታ (ትሪኮሞኒስስ) ብዙውን ጊዜ በነባር ፣ ውጤታማ ነጠላ-መጠን ወይም ባለብዙ መጠን የአንቲባዮቲክ መድኃኒቶች ይድናል። ለቫይረስ የአባላዘር በሽታዎች (ኤች አይ ቪ, ኤች ኤስ ቪ እና ኤችቲኤልቪ-1) በጣም ውጤታማ የሆኑት መድሃኒቶች የፀረ-ቫይረስ ወይም ፀረ-ካንሰር መድሐኒቶች (በኤችቲኤልቪ-1 ሁኔታ) ምንም እንኳን እነዚህን ሶስት በሽታዎች ማዳን ባይችሉም የበሽታዎችን አካሄድ ማስተካከል ይችላሉ። የፀረ-ተህዋሲያን ተከላካይነት( ኤኤምአር) በፆታ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፉ በሽታዎችን በተለይም ጨብጥን ለማከም ጥቅም ላይ ለሚውሉ አንቲባዮቲኮች በቅርብ ዓመታት በፍጥነት ጨምሯል እናም የተሳካ የህክምና ውጤቶችን ቀንሷል። ከጎኖኮካል ኤኤምአር የክትትል ፕሮግራም( ጂኤኤስፒ) የተገኙ ውጤቶች ከፍተኛ መጠን ያለው የኪኖሎን መቋቋም፣ የአዚትሮሚሲን መቋቋም እና የተራዘመ ስፔክትረም ሴፋሎስፖሪን የመቋቋም አዝማሚያዎችን ያሳያሉ። የጨብጥ ለተራዘመ ስፔክትረም ሴፋሎስፖሪን የመጋለጥ ችሎታ መቀነስ፣ ከፔኒሲሊን፣ ሱልፎናሚድ፣ ቴትራሳይክሊን፣ ኪኖሎን እና ማክሮላይድ ጋር የተቋቋመ ከፍተኛ የመቋቋም ደረጃ ጨብጥ ብዙ መድኃኒት የመቋቋም ችሎታ ያለው ተህዋስያን ያደርገዋል። ሌሎች በፆታ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፉ ኢንፌክሽኖችን የመከላከል እና ፈጣን ህክምናን ወሳኝ የሚያደርጉ ሌሎች የፆታ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፉ ኢንፌክሽኖችን የመከላከል አቅም ቢኖርም በጣም የተለመደ አይደለም። የአባላዘር በሽታዎችን በበቂ ሁኔታ ለማከም ትክክለኛውን መጠን እና ለተወሰነ የአባላዘር በሽታ በቂ ህክምና ወይም ፈውስ ለማረጋገጥ እና ፀረ ተሕዋስያን የመቋቋም እድገትን ለመከላከል ተገቢውን መጠን እና የቆይታ ጊዜ በመያዝና ተገቢውን ፀረ ተሕዋስያን መድሃኒት መውሰድ አስፈላጊ ነው።
translations.reference: Latent syphilis is asymptomatic and characterized by positive syphilis serology. Trichomoniasis: The predominant symptoms include abnormal vaginal discharge with redness of the vulva, itching and painful intercourse. Genital herpes simplex virus (HSV) HSV most commonly presents as painful sores, vesicles or ulcerations on the external genitalia and mouth. Symptomatic genital HSV is a lifelong condition that can be characterized by frequent symptomatic recurrences. Human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) Generally asymptomatic, the chronic form of HTLV-1 can cause severe disease, including adult T-cell leukaemia/lymphoma (ATL) and a progressive nervous system condition known as HTLV-1-associated myelopathy or tropical spastic paraparesis (HAM/TSP). Treatment: Effective treatment is currently available for several STIs. Three bacterial STIs (chlamydia, gonorrhoea and syphilis) and one parasitic STI (trichomoniasis) are usually curable with existing, effective single-dose or multiple-dose regimens of antibiotics. For viral STIs (HIV, HSV and HTLV-1), the most effective medications available are antivirals or anti-cancer drugs (in the case of HTLV-1), that can modulate the course of the diseases, though they cannot cure these three diseases. Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) to antibiotics used to treat STIs, in particular gonorrhoea, has increased rapidly in recent years and has reduced successful treatment outcomes. Results from the current Gonococcal AMR Surveillance Programme (GASP) show trends of high rates of quinolone resistance, increasing azithromycin resistance and emerging resistance of extended-spectrum cephalosporins. The emergence of decreased susceptibility of gonorrhoea to extended-spectrum cephalosporins, together with established high-levels of resistance to penicillins, sulphonamides, tetracyclines, quinolones and macrolides make gonorrhoea a multidrug-resistant organism. AMR for other STIs, though less common, also exists, making prevention and prompt treatment critical. To adequately treat STIs it is important to take the appropriate antimicrobials, with the correct dose and duration for the specific STI to ensure adequate treatment or cure, and to prevent the development of antimicrobial resistance.
translations.hypothesis: Chlamydia is a sexually transmitted infection (STI) that is often asymptomatic, meaning it may not cause any noticeable symptoms. However, it can be diagnosed through a positive chlamydia test. The main symptoms of chlamydia include unusual vaginal discharge, pain or burning during urination, and painful intercourse. Herpes simplex virus (HSV) is another common STI that can cause painful sores or blisters on the genitals, buttocks, or thighs. The symptoms of HSV vary depending on whether it is a primary or recurrent infection. Human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) is a slow-acting virus that can cause a rare type of cancer called adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma (ATL). Treatment for chlamydia and other STIs typically involves antibiotics. For bacterial STIs, such as chlamydia, gonorrhea, and syphilis, single or multiple doses of antibiotics are usually effective. For viral STIs, such as HIV, herpes, and HTLV-1, antiviral or anticancer medications may be used. While these medications cannot cure these infections, they can help to control the symptoms and prevent the spread of the infection. In recent years, there has been a growing interest in using antibiotics to treat STIs, including chlamydia. This approach has been shown to be effective in some cases, but it is important to note that antibiotic resistance is a growing problem.

translations.source: ፈንጣጣ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ ፈንጣጣ የኦርቶፖክስ ቫይረስ ቤተሰብ አባል በሆነው በቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ የሚከሰት አጣዳፊ ተላላፊ በሽታ ነው። ይህ በሽታ በሰው ልጆች ዘንድ ከሚታወቁት እጅግ አስከፊ በሽታዎች አንዱ ሲሆን ከመጥፋቱ በፊት በሚሊዮኖች ለሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ሞት ምክንያት ሆኗል። ቢያንስ ለ 3000 ዓመታት እንደኖረ ይታመናል። በ1796 በኤድዋርድ ጄነር የተፈጠረው የፈንጣጣ ክትባት የመጀመሪያው የተሳካ ክትባት ነው። ከዚህ በፊት የከብት ፈንጣጣ ያጋጠማቸው የወተት ጠባቂዎች የፈንጣጣ በሽታ እንዳላጋጠማቸው በመግለጽ ተመሳሳይ ክትባት በሌሎች ሰዎች ላይም ሊሠራ እንደሚችል አሳይቷል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በ1967 ፈንጣጣን ለማጥፋት የተጠናከረ እቅድ አውጥቷል። ለብዙ አመታት በአለም ዙሪያ ሰፊ የሆነ የክትባት እና ክትትል ተካሂዷል። የመጨረሻው የታወቀ የተፈጥሮ ጒዳይ በሶማሊያ በ 1977 ነበር. እ.ኤ.አ. በ 1980 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፈንጣጣ መጥፋቱን አወጀ። ይህ ልዩነት የተገኘበት ብቸኛው ተላላፊ በሽታ ነው። ይህ በታሪክ ውስጥ በጣም ከሚታወቁ እና ጥልቅ የህዝብ ጤና ስኬቶች መካከል አንዱ ሆኖ ይቆያል። ምልክቶች፡ የፈንጣጣ የመጀመሪያ ምልክቶች ከፍተኛ ትኩሳት፣ ድካም እና ከባድ የጀርባ ህመም፣ እና ብዙ ጊዜ የሆድ ህመም እና ማስታወክ ያካትታሉ። ከሁለት እስከ 3 ቀናት በኋላ ቫይረሱ በጠራ ፈሳሽ የተሞሉ እብጠቶች ያሉበት ባህሪይ ሽፍታ ይፈጥራል፣ ይህም በኋላ መግል ይሞላል እና በመጨረሻም የሚደርቅ እና የሚወድቅ ቅርፊት ይፈጥራል። ሽፍታው በፊት እና በእጆች ላይ ይጀምራል፤ ከዚያም ወደ ቀሪው የሰውነት ክፍል ይሰራጫል። ጉዳቶች በአፍንጫ እና በአፍ ሽፋን ውስጥ ይከሰታሉ እና ከተፈጠሩ በኋላ ብዙም ሳይቆይ ቍስል ይፈጥራሉ። ፈንጣጣ ከሰው ወደ ሰው የሚተላለፈው የበሽታው ምልክት ካላቸው ሰዎች ጋር በቅርበት በሚገናኙበት ወቅት ተላላፊ በሆኑ ጠብታዎች ነው፣ ወይም በአንዳንድ ሁኔታዎች በተበከሉ ልብሶች እና አልጋዎች። ከተጋለጡ በኋላ ከ7-17 ቀናት ውስጥ የመራቢያ ጊዜ አለው እና ትኩሳት ከተፈጠረ በኋላ ብቻ ተላላፊ ይሆናል። የመጨረሻው እከክ እስኪወድቅ ድረስ ሰዎች ተላላፊ ሆነው ይቆያሉ። ፈንጣጣ እስከ 30% የሚሆኑት ጉዳዮች ገዳይ ነበር።የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የፈንጣጣ መታወቂያ ካርድ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፡- ከመጥፋት በኋላ ያለው ጊዜ የተገለፀው በረጅም እና ውስብስብ ክርክር የመጨረሻ ቀሪ የቀጥታ ቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ክምችት መጥፋት ላይ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 1996 ፣ በ 49 ኛው የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ ፣ አባል ሀገራት ይህ ክምችት በሰኔ 1999 እንዲወድም ወስነዋል ፣ እና ትናንሽ ናሙናዎች ለምርምር ዓላማዎች በሁለት በተመረጡ አስተማማኝ ላቦራቶሪዎች ውስጥ ብቻ እንዲቀመጡ መፍቀድ ነው - አንድ በዩናይትድ ስቴትስ እና አንድ በሩሲያ ፌዴሬሽን። አሁን ያለውን የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ክምችት ጊዜያዊ ማቆየት ለተጨማሪ አስፈላጊ ምርምር ጥቅም ላይ ይውላል፣ ይህም በቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ምርምር አማካሪ ኮሚቴ ቁጥጥር ስር ነው። ይህ ቡድን በየአመቱ ይሰበሰባል። በአለም የጤና ድርጅት ዋና መሥሪያ ቤት የሚገኘው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፈንጣጣ ጽሕፈት ቤት ሁሉንም ፈንጣጣ ማጥፋት-ተያያዥ እና ድህረ ማጥፋት እንቅስቃሴዎችን ያስተባብራል። እነዚህ እንደ ክትባት እና ፀረ-ቫይረስ ያሉ የመከላከያ እርምጃዎችን ለማዘጋጀት ከቀጥታ ቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ጋር የተፈቀደ ምርምርን መቆጣጠርን ያካትታሉ።
translations.reference: Smallpox Overview: Smallpox is an acute contagious disease caused by the variola virus, a member of the orthopoxvirus family. It was one of the most devastating diseases known to humanity and caused millions of deaths before it was eradicated. It is believed to have existed for at least 3000 years. The smallpox vaccine, created by Edward Jenner in 1796, was the first successful vaccine to be developed. He observed that milkmaids who previously had caught cowpox did not catch smallpox and showed that a similar inoculation could be used to prevent smallpox in other people. The World Health Organization launched an intensified plan to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread immunization and surveillance were conducted around the world for several years. The last known natural case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to achieve this distinction. This remains among the most notable and profound public health successes in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of smallpox include high fever, fatigue and severe back pain, and less often, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later the virus produces a characteristic rash with bumps full of a clear liquid, which later fill with pus and finally develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. Lesions develop in the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and ulcerate soon after formation. Smallpox is transmitted from person to person via infective droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in some cases through contaminated clothing and bedding. It has an incubation period of 7–17 days after exposure and only becomes infectious once a fever develops. People remain infectious until the last scabs fall off. Smallpox was fatal in up to 30% of cases.WHO smallpox recognition card WHO response: The period since eradication has been defined by a lengthy and complex debate focussed on the destruction of the last remaining stocks of live variola virus. In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals.
translations.hypothesis: Smallpox is a contagious disease caused by the variola virus, a member of the orthopoxvirus family. It is one of the most devastating diseases in human history, causing millions of deaths before its eradication in the late 20th century. The first confirmed case of smallpox was recorded in ancient Egypt, around 1500 BC. The disease spread throughout the world, causing widespread death and suffering. In the 18th century, smallpox was a major public health problem in Europe and North America. The first successful smallpox vaccine was developed by Edward Jenner in 1796. Jenner observed that milkmaids who had contracted cowpox, a mild form of the disease, were immune to smallpox. He developed a vaccine by inoculating people with cowpox, which then protected them from smallpox. The smallpox vaccine was widely adopted and helped to eradicate the disease from most of the world. The World Health Organization (WHO) launched a global campaign to eradicate smallpox in 1967. The campaign involved widespread vaccination programs and surveillance efforts. The last known case of smallpox was in Somalia in 1977. The WHO declared smallpox eradicated in 1980.

translations.source: ከበሽታው ማጥፋት ዘመን ጀምሮ ደህንነታቸው የተጠበቀ ክትባቶች እና ልዩ ህክምናዎች ለፈንጣጣ እና ለተዛማጅ በሽታዎች እንደ ዝንጀሮ ፈንጣጣ ተዘጋጅተዋል። ጽህፈት ቤቱ ለአደጋ ጊዜ ዝግጁነት ሀላፊነት ያለው ሲሆን በሽታው እንደገና ሊያገረሽ በማይቻልበት ሁኔታ የፈንጣጣ ክትባት የድንገተኛ ጊዜ ክምችትን ይቆጣጠራል። ጽሕፈት ቤቱ በዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ በተደነገገው መሠረት በተፈቀደላቸው የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ማከማቻዎች ላይ የሕይወት ደኅንነት እና የባዮሴኪዩሪቲ ቁጥጥርን ይቆጣጠራል፣ እና ለዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የበላይ አካላት አመታዊ ሪፖርትን ያረጋግጣል። የፈንጣጣ ጽሕፈት ቤት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የፈንጣጣ በሽታን ታሪክ በመጠበቅ እና ፈንጣጣን በተሳካ ሁኔታ በማጥፋት የተገኙትን ትምህርቶች በመመዝገብ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት መዝገብ ቤትን ይደግፋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የፈንጣጣ ጽሕፈት ቤት አሁን ያለው ተግባር እና ኃላፊነቶች የሚከተሉትን ያጠቃልላል፡ የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ምርምር እና የመከላከያ እርምጃዎች እድገት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ምርምር (ኤሲቪቪአር) አማካሪ ኮሚቴ አመታዊ ስብሰባዎችን የሚያደራጁ የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ምርምር እና የመከላከያ እርምጃዎች እድገት በ ኤሲቪቪአር በኩል ከቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ጋር ለመስራት የምርምር ሃሳቦች ግምገማን ማስተባበር ከሁለቱ የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ማከማቻዎች ጋር ግንኙነቶችን ማስተዳደር (እንዲሁም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የትብብር ማዕከላት ናቸው) ከተመራማሪዎች የቀረበውን የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ዲ ኤን ኤ ከ 2 ማከማቻዎች በልዩ ህጎች ውስጥ የሚቀርቡ ጥያቄዎችን መቅዳት ፣ ማቀናበር እና መከታተል በየሁለት ዓመቱ የሕይወት ደኅንነት እና የባዮሴኪዩሪቲ ምርመራዎችን በ2ቱ ዓለም አቀፍ የቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ማከማቻዎች ማካሄድ በቫሪዮላ ቫይረስ ዲ ኤን ኤ እና ተዛማጅ ጉዳዮች ላይ ምርምርን በተመለከተ መደበኛ መመሪያን ማዳበር የአደጋ ጊዜ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ ለ ኦርቶፖክስ ቫይረሶች የሕክምና መከላከያ ዘዴዎችን በማዳበር እና አጠቃቀም ላይ መደበኛ መመሪያን ማስተባበር እና ማዘጋጀት የዝግጅት እና ምላሽ ስልቶችን እና የዝግጅት እርምጃዎችን ለመተግበር መሣሪያዎችን ማዘጋጀት። የፈንጣጣ ዓለም አቀፍ የክትባት ክምችትን መጠበቅ እና ማስተዳደር ለ ኦርቶፖክስ ፍለጋ እና ምርመራ የላብራቶሪ አውታረመረብ ልማትን ማስተባበር ለሰው ልጅ የዝንጀሮ በሽታ እና ሌሎች ኦርቶፖክስ ቫይረሶች መደበኛ መመሪያ እና ምላሽ ስልቶችን ማዘጋጀት የአቅም ግንባታ እና የሥልጠና ቁሳቁሶችን መደገፍ ለአለም አቀፍ የጤና ደህንነት ተነሳሽነት አስተዋፅኦ ማድረግ የአለም አቀፍ የፈንጣጣ ታሪክን መጠበቅ የፈንጣጣ ወረርሽኝን የማጥፋት መርሃ ግብር ማህደሮችን ከአለም ጤና ድርጅት ማህደሮች እና ከአለም ጤና ድርጅት ቤተ-መጽሐፍት ጋር በመተባበር በበላይነት መከታተል። ፈንጣጣ ማጥፋትን በተመለከተ የፖሊሲ ውይይቶችን እና የመታሰቢያ ዝግጅቶችን ይመራል ለዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሥራ አመራር ቦርድ እና ለዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ በየዓመቱ ሪፖርት ማድረግ ጽሕፈት ቤቱ ለእነዚህ ተግባራት እና ለጊዜያዊ ክንውኖች እንደ ቴክኒካል ምክክር እና ስልጠና የመሳሰሉ የገንዘብ ድጋፎችን ያንቀሳቅሳል እና ያስተዳድራል።
translations.reference: Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.hypothesis: The department is responsible for developing and implementing effective strategies to prevent and control smallpox, a deadly disease that has been eradicated worldwide. It maintains a stockpile of smallpox vaccine and other essential medical supplies to respond to outbreaks. The department also works with the World Health Organization (WHO) to ensure that all countries have access to the necessary resources to prevent and control smallpox. In addition, the department conducts research on smallpox and other orthopoxviruses, and it provides technical assistance to other countries in their efforts to prevent and control these diseases. The department's work is essential to ensuring that smallpox is never reintroduced to the world.

translations.source: የእባብ ንክሻ መመረዝ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ የእባብ ንክሻ መመረዝ በመርዛማ እባብ ንክሻ ምክንያት የሚመጣ ለሕይወት አስጊ የሆነ በሽታ ነው። የተወሰኑ የእባብ ዝርያዎች በመከላከያነት መርዝ የመርጨት ችሎታ ስላላቸው መርዝ በአይኖች ላይ በመርጨት መርዝ ሊከሰት ይችላል። የእባብ ንክሻን ለመቆጣጠር ያለፉት ጥረቶች በቂ ባልሆነ መልኩ የተበታተኑ እና ትክክለኛ ያልሆኑ የኢፒዲሚዮሎጂ መረጃዎችን አፍርተዋል። ብዙ ተጎጂዎች ወደ ጤና ጣቢያዎች ወይም ሆስፒታሎች አይሄዱም እና ይልቁንም በባህላዊ ህክምናዎች ላይ ጥገኛ ናቸው። ነገር ግን በዓመት ከ4.5-5.4 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች በእባብ ይነደፋሉ። ከዚህ ውስጥ 1.8-2.7 ሚሊዮን ክሊኒካዊ ህመም ያጋጥማቸዋል እና ከ 81 000 እስከ 138 000 የሚሆኑት በችግር ይሞታሉ ። ከፍተኛ ስጋት ያለባቸው ቡድኖች የገጠር ግብርና ሰራተኞች፣ እረኞች፣ አሳ አጥማጆች፣ አዳኞች፣ የሚሰሩ ልጆች፣ በደንብ ባልተገነቡ ቤቶች ውስጥ የሚኖሩ እና የትምህርት እና የጤና አጠባበቅ ውስንነት ያላቸውን ያጠቃልላል። በወጣቶች መካከል የበሽታ እና የሞት መጠን በብዛት ይከሰታሉ እና በልጆች ላይ ከፍተኛ ሞት ይደርስባቸዋል። በተጨማሪም ሴቶች በአንዳንድ ባህሎች የህክምና አገልግሎት ለማግኘት እንቅፋቶች ያጋጥሟቸዋል እና ነፍሰ ጡር እናቶች በጣም የተጋለጡ ናቸው። በበርካታ ክልሎች በተለይም ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ አገራት ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ውጤታማ የሆነ የፀረ-መርዝ ህክምና ተደራሽነትን የሚገድብ ቀጣይነት ያለው ቀውስ በባህላዊ መድኃኒት በኩል እርዳታ ለመፈለግ ቅድመ ሁኔታ እንዲኖር አስተዋጽኦ የሚያደርግ አንዱ ምክንያት ነው። ምልክቶች፡ ከመርዛማ እባቦች ንክሻ ወይም የተረጨ መርዝ የተለያዩ አጣዳፊ እና ከባድ የሕክምና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎችን ያስከትላል። ከተለያዩ የእባቦች አይነት የሚመጡ እባቦች የተለያዩ ምልክቶችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ፣ አንዳንዶቹ ከሌሎቹ የበለጠ ከባድ ናቸው። ይህ ትክክለኛውን ፀረ-መርዛማ ንጥረ ነገር ማዘጋጀት ቀጣይነት ያለው ችግር ያደርገዋል። መርዛማነት ከባድ ሽባነትን ሊያስከትል ይችላል ይህም መተንፈስን ሊያግድ ይችላል፣ ወዲያውኑ የህክምና እርዳታ ወሳኝ ያደርገዋል። ሰዎች ወደ ገዳይ ደም መፍሰስ ወይም ወደማይቀለበስ የኩላሊት ውድቀት ሊያመራ የሚችል የደም መፍሰስ ችግር ሊያጋጥማቸው ይችላል። ከባድ የአካባቢ ሕብረ ሕዋሳት መጥፋትም ሊከሰት ይችላል፣ ይህም ወደ ዘላቂ የአካል ጉዳት እና አልፎ ተርፎም የእጅ እግር መቆረጥ ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ልጆች ከአዋቂዎች ይልቅ ዝቅተኛ የሰውነት ክብደት ምክንያት ለከባድ ተጽእኖዎች ከፍተኛ ተጋላጭነት አላቸው። አብዛኛዎቹ ሞት እና በእባቦች ንክሻ ምክንያት የሚመጡ ከባድ መዘዞች ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ውጤታማ ፀረ-ተህዋሲያን በይበልጥ በስፋት የሚገኙ እና ተደራሽ በማድረግ በተለይም ከፍተኛ ተጋላጭነት ባለባቸው አካባቢዎች ሙሉ በሙሉ መከላከል ይቻላል። ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያላቸው የእባብ ፀረ መርዞች የእባብ ንክሻ መርዛማ ውጤቶችን ለመከላከል ወይም ለመቀልበስ ብቸኛው ውጤታማ ህክምና ናቸው። ሕክምና፡ የእባብ ፀረ መርዞች አብዛኛዎቹን የእባብ ንክሳት መከላትን ለመከላከል ወይም ለመቀልበስ ውጤታማ ህክምናዎች ናቸው እና በ WHO አስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶች ዝርዝር ውስጥ ተካትተዋል። የእነዚህ ፀረ-ተህዋሲያን መገኘት እና ተደራሽነት በማህበረሰቦች እና በጤና ባለሙያዎች መካከል በአንደኛ ደረጃ የመከላከያ ዘዴዎች ላይ ግንዛቤን ከማሳደግ ጋር, በእባብ ንክሻ ምክንያት አስከፊ መዘዞችን እና ሞትን ለመገደብ የተሻሉ መንገዶች ናቸው። መርዛማ ነው ተብሎ በሚታሰብ እባብ ከተነከሱ በኋላ የሚከተሉትን እርምጃዎች ይውሰዱ:- ንክሻው ከተከሰተበት ቦታ ወዲያውኑ ይራቁ። እብጠት ቢፈጠር ጉዳት እንዳይደርስበት በተነከሰው የሰውነት ክፍል ዙሪያ ያለውን ጥብቅ ነገር ያስወግዱ።
translations.reference: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments. However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable. An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical. People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites. Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.hypothesis: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by parasites transmitted through the bite of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. The parasites multiply in the human liver and then invade red blood cells, causing the characteristic symptoms of malaria. Malaria is a major public health problem in many parts of the world, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa. The disease is transmitted through the bite of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. The mosquitoes become infected when they bite a person with malaria. The parasites then multiply in the mosquito's gut and migrate to its salivary glands. When the infected mosquito bites another person, it injects the parasites into the person's bloodstream. Malaria is most commonly treated with artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs). ACTs are effective because they kill the parasites in both the liver and the blood. However, ACTs are not always available in all parts of the world. In some areas, people may have to rely on traditional medicines to treat malaria. Traditional medicines can be effective, but they are not always safe. It is important to talk to a doctor before using any traditional medicine to treat malaria. Malaria is a serious disease that can be fatal. However, with proper treatment, most people with malaria can recover completely.

translations.source: አብዛኞቹ መርዛማ የእባቦች ንክሻ ወዲያውኑ ሞትን ስለማያስከትል ተጎጂውን አረጋጋው። ግለሰቡን ሙሉ በሙሉ ማንቀሳቀስ እና በተቻለ ፍጥነት ግለሰቡን ወደ ጤና ተቋም ማጓጓዝ በንክሻ ቦታ ላይ የግፊት ንጣፍ መጫን በአንዳንድ ሁኔታዎች ተስማሚ ሊሆን ይችላል። ከባህላዊ የመጀመሪያ እርዳታ ዘዴዎች ወይም ከዕፅዋት የተቀመሙ መድኃኒቶችን ያስወግዱ። ፓራሲታሞል በአካባቢው ህመም ሊሰጥ ይችላል (ይህም ከባድ ሊሆን ይችላል)። ማስታወክ ሊከሰት ይችላል፣ ስለዚህ ሰውየውን በግራ ጎናቸው በማገገሚያ ቦታ ላይ ያድርጉት። የአየር መንገዱን እና አተነፋፈስን በቅርበት ይቆጣጠሩ እና አስፈላጊ ከሆነ እንደገና ለማደስ ዝግጁ ይሁኑ
translations.reference: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe). Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.hypothesis: First, reassure the victim that the snakebite is unlikely to be fatal. It may be appropriate to apply a tourniquet to the affected limb and transport the person to a medical facility as quickly as possible. Remove any traditional first-aid methods or herbal remedies. Paracetamol may cause nausea, so avoid giving it to the person. If vomiting occurs, place them on their side to prevent choking. Closely monitor their breathing and be prepared to administer artificial respiration if necessary.

translations.source: በአፈር የሚተላለፉ ሄልሚንቲየስ በሽታዎች። አጠቃላይ እይታ በአፈር ውስጥ የሚተላለፉ የሄልሚንት ኢንፌክሽኖች( ኤስቲኤች) በዓለም ዙሪያ በጣም ከተለመዱት ኢንፌክሽኖች መካከል ናቸው እናም በጣም ድሀ እና በጣም የተጐዱ ማህበረሰቦችን ይጎዳሉ። በሰው ሠራሽ ፍሳሽ ውስጥ በሚገኙ እንቁላሎች አማካኝነት የሚተላለፉ ሲሆን ይህ ደግሞ የንፅህና አጠባበቅ ጒድለት ባለባቸው አካባቢዎች ያለውን አፈር ያበላሻል። ሰዎችን የሚያጠቁት ዋና ዋና ዝርያዎች ክብ ትል (አሲካሪስ ላምብሪኮይዴስ)፣ ዊፕትል (ትሪቹሪስ ትሪቺዩራ) እና መንጠቆዎች (ኔካቶር አሜሪካናስ እና አንሲሎስቶማ ዱዶኔሌ) ናቸው። የአንጀት ትሎች የአንጀት ምልክቶችን( የሆድ ድርቀት፣ የሆድ ሕመም)፣ አጠቃላይ ህመምን እና ድክመትን ጨምሮ ሰፋ ያሉ ምልክቶችን ያስከትላሉ። ሃክዎርሞች የደም ማነስ የሚያስከትሉ ሥር የሰደደ የአንጀት ደም ማጣት ያስከትላሉ። በአፈር የሚተላለፉ ሄልሚንቶች የሚተላለፉት በበሽታው በተያዙ ሰዎች ፍሳሽ ውስጥ በሚተላለፉ እንቁላሎች ነው። ጎልማሳ ትሎች በየቀኑ በሺዎች የሚቈጠሩ እንቁላሎችን በሚያፈሩበት አንጀት ውስጥ ይኖራሉ። በቂ የንፅህና አጠባበቅ በሌለባቸው አካባቢዎች እነዚህ እንቁላሎች መሬቱን ይበክላሉ። ይህ በበርካታ መንገዶች ሊከሰት ይችላል:. ከአትክልቶች ጋር የተያያዙ እንቁላሎች አትክልቶቹ በጥንቃቄ ሳይበስሉ፣ ሳይታጠቡ ወይም የተላጠ ሲበሉ ይወሰዳሉ። እንቁላሎች ከተበከሉ የውኃ ምንጮች ውስጥ ወደ ውስጥ ይገባሉ። እንቁላሎቹ በተበከለው አፈር ውስጥ የሚጫወቱ እና እጃቸውን ሳይታጠቡ ወደ አፋቸው በሚያስገቡ ልጆች ይዋጣሉ። ምልክቶች፡ የጤና እክል የሚከሰተው በተያዙት ትሎች ብዛት ጋር የተያያዘ ነው። የብርሃን ጥንካሬ ኢንፌክሽን ያለባቸው ሰዎች( ጥቂት ትሎች) ብዙውን ጊዜ በበሽታው አይሰቃዩም። ከባድ ኢንፌክሽኖች የተለያዩ ምልክቶችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ። የአንጀት መገለጫዎችን( የሆድ ድርቀት እና የሆድ ሕመም)፣ የተመጣጠነ ምግብ እጥረት፣ አጠቃላይ ሕመም እና ድክመት እንዲሁም የዕድገት እና የአካል እድገት መዛባት ጨምሮ የተለያዩ ምልክቶችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ። በጣም ከፍተኛ ኃይለኛ ኢንፌክሽኖች በቀዶ ሕክምና ሊታከሙ የሚገባውን የአንጀት መዘጋት ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ኤስ ስተርኮራሊስ የቆዳ እና የጨጓራ-አንጀት በሽታዎችን ሊያስከትል ይችላል። እንዲሁም በልጆች ላይ ከከባድ የተመጣጠነ ምግብ እጥረት ጋር የተቆራኘ መሆኑ ይታወቃል። የአስተናጋጅ በሽታ የመከላከል አቅም ሲቀንስ ፓራሳይቱ የከፍተኛ ኢንፌክሽን/ ስርጭት ሲንድሮም ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ይህም በአፋጣኝ እና በአግባቡ ካልተፈወሰ ሁል ጊዜም ለሞት የሚዳርግ እና ህክምና ቢደረግም ብዙውን ጊዜ ለሞት የሚዳርግ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2001 የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ ልዑካን በአንድ ድምፅ (ዳብሊውኤችኤ54.19) ሥር የሰደዱ አገሮች ትላትሎችን በተለይም ስኪስቶሶሚያሲስ እና በአፈር የሚተላለፉ ሄልሚንትስ በትል መከላከል እንዲጀምሩ የሚያሳስብ ውሳኔ (ዳብሊውኤችኤ54.19) በአንድ ድምፅ አጽድቀዋል። በአፈር የሚተላለፉ የሄልሚንት ኢንፌክሽኖችን ለመቈጣጠር የሚያስችል ስትራቴጂ በኤንደሚክ አካባቢዎች የሚኖሩ ተጋላጭ የሆኑ ሰዎችን በየጊዜው በማከም በሽታን መቈጣጠር ነው። ለአደጋ የተጋለጡ ሰዎች፡- የቅድመ ትምህርት ቤት ልጆች እድሜያቸው ለትምህርት የደረሱ ልጆች የመራቢያ ዕድሜ ላይ ያሉ ሴቶች (በሁለተኛው እና በሦስተኛው ወር የወር አበባ ውስጥ እርጉዝ ሴቶችን እና ጡት የሚያጠቡ ሴቶችን ጨምሮ) እንደ ሻይ ሰብሳቢዎች ወይም ማዕድን ቆፋሪዎች ባሉ የተወሰኑ ከፍተኛ አደጋ ያላቸው ሙያዎች ውስጥ ያሉ አዋቂዎች
translations.reference: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled; eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development. Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas. People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.hypothesis: Parasitic worms transmitted through soil: This refers to infections caused by parasites that live in the soil and are transmitted to humans through contact with contaminated soil. These infections are common in areas with poor sanitation and hygiene, and they can affect people of all ages. The most common types of soil-transmitted parasites include: Ascaris lumbricoides (roundworms), Trichuris trichiura (whipworms), and hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). These parasites can cause a variety of symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss, and anemia. In severe cases, they can lead to malnutrition and stunted growth. The parasites are transmitted through the ingestion of eggs or larvae in contaminated soil. This can happen when people eat contaminated food, or when they come into contact with contaminated soil and then touch their mouths. Symptoms: The severity of the symptoms depends on the number of parasites present. People with mild infections may not experience any symptoms. However, people with more severe infections may experience a variety of symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss, and anemia. In some cases, the infection can lead to malnutrition and stunted growth. Treatment: Treatment for soil-transmitted parasites typically involves taking medication to kill the parasites. The specific medication used will depend on the type of parasite. Prevention: The best way to prevent soil-transmitted parasites is to practice good hygiene. This includes washing your hands frequently, especially after using the toilet and before eating. It is also important to avoid eating food that has not been properly cooked or washed.

translations.source: ሕክምና: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የሚመክረው መድኃኒት አልቤንዳዞል( 400 ሚግ) እና ሜቤንዳዞል( 500 ሚግ) ውጤታማ፣ ርካሽ እና የህክምና ባልሆኑ ሰዎች( ለምሳሌ መምህራን) በቀላሉ የሚተገበሩ ናቸው። እነዚህ መድኃኒቶች በሰፊው የደኅንነት ምርመራ የተደረገባቸው ሲሆን በሚሊዮኖች በሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ላይ ጥቅም ላይ የዋሉ ሲሆን የጐንዮሽ ጉዳቶችም አነስተኛ ናቸው። ሁለቱም አልበንዳዞል እና ሜበንዳዞል በሁሉም የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴሮች በአለም ጤና ድርጅት በኩል ለትምህርት ቤት ዕድሜያችውለደረሱ ሕፃናት ህክምና ይሰጣሉ። ኤስ ስቴርኮራሊስን ለመቈጣጠር አይቨርሜክቲን እ.ኤ.አ ከ 2021 ጀምሮ በተመጣጣኝ ዋጋ ይገኛል ተብሎ ይጠበቃል።
translations.reference: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: The World Health Organization recommends Albendazole (400 mg) and Mebendazole (500 mg) as effective, affordable, and easily administered medications, even for non-medical personnel (e.g., teachers). These medications have been widely studied and used by millions of people, with minimal side effects. Both Albendazole and Mebendazole are recommended by health ministries worldwide for the treatment of intestinal parasites in school-aged children. Ivermectin has also been found to be an effective and affordable treatment for S. stercoralis, and is expected to become more widely available from 2021 onwards.

translations.source: የሞተ ልደት አጠቃላይ እይታ: ከ 28 ሳምንታት እርግዝና በኋላ፣ ግን ከመወለዱ በፊት ወይም በሚወለድበት ጊዜ የሚሞት ሕፃን እንደ የሞተ ልደት ተደርጐ ይወሰዳል። በየዓመቱ ወደ 2 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ የሞተ ልደቶች አሉ። በየ 16 ሰከንዶች ደግሞ አንድ።. ከ 40% በላይ የሚሆኑት የሟች ልደቶች የሚከሰቱት በወሊድ ወቅት ነው። ይህ ኪሳራ በወሊድ ጊዜ በተሻሻለ ጥራት እና በአክብሮት እንክብካቤ ሊወገድ የሚችል መደበኛ ክትትል እና አስፈላጊ ሆኖ ሲገኝ የድንገተኛ ጊዜ የወሊድ አገልግሎት ማግኘትን ይጨምራል። በእርግዝና ወይም በወሊድ ወቅት የሞተ ልጅ መውለድ በዓለምአቀፍ አጀንዳዎች፣ ፖሊሲዎች እና በገንዘብ በተደገፉ ፕሮግራሞች በቂ ትኩረት ያልተሰጠው አሳዛኝ ክስተት ነው። በተለይም ለሴቶች እና ለቤተሰቦቻቸው የስነልቦና ወጪዎች አሉ። ለምሳሌ የእናቶች ድብርት፣ የገንዘብ መዘዞች እና ኢኮኖሚያዊ መዘዞች፣ እንዲሁም ነቀፋ እና ክልከላዎች ናቸው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ2014 የአለም ጤና ጉባኤ በ2030 በእያንዳንዱ ሀገር ከ1,000 አጠቃላይ የወሊድ 12 ወይም ከዚያ በታች የሶስተኛ ወር (ዘግይቶ) የሟች ልደትን የሚያጠቃልለውን እያንዳንዱ አዲስ የተወለደ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር (ኢኤንኤፒ) አጽድቋል። በ 2021 139 በዋናነት ከፍተኛ ገቢ ያላቸው እና ከፍተኛ መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸው አገራት ይህንን ግብ አሟልተዋል። ነገር ግን ተጨማሪ ጥረቶች ካልተደረጉ 56 አገራት እያንዳንዳቸው የኢኤንኤፒን ግብ በ 2030 አያሟሉም። አሁን ያለው አዝማሚያ ከቀጠለ 15,9 ሚሊዮን ሕፃናት ሞተው ይወለዳሉ። ከእነዚህ ውስጥ ግማሽ ያህሉ( 7,7 ሚሊዮን - 48በመቶው) የሚሆኑት ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ አገሮች ይወለዳሉ። መንስኤዎች: በእርግዝና እና በወሊድ ወቅት ጥራት ያለው የጤና እንክብካቤ ሲኖር አብዛኛዎቹ የሟች ሕፃናት መከላከል ይቻላል። የሟች መወለድ ዋነኛ መንስኤዎች ከእርግዝና እና ከወሊድ ጋር የተዛመዱ ችግሮች፣ ረዘም ያለ እርግዝና፣ የእናቶች ኢንፌክሽኖች እንደ ወባ፣ ሲፊሊስ እና ኤች አይ ቪ. የእናትነት ህመሞች በተለይም የደም ግፊት እና የስኳር በሽታ እንዲሁም የጽንስ እድገት መገደብ( ያልተወለደ ሕፃን የዕድገቱን አቅም ማሳካት በማይችልበት እና ስለሆነም ከሚገባው በላይ ትንሽ በሚሆንበት ጊዜ) በከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ በሀገር አቀፍ ደረጃ ከተመዘገቡት የሟች ሕፃናት ውስጥ ከ 10በመቶ በታች ለሆኑት የወሊድ መዛባት ተጠያቂ ናቸው። እና በምርመራዎች ውስንነት ምክንያት ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አካባቢዎች ያለው ትክክለኛ መጠን አይታወቅም። የእናቱ እድሜና ማጨስ ጋር የተያያዙ ምክንያቶችም የእናትነት በሽታ እና የሞተ ልደት አደጋን ሊጨምሩ ይችላሉ። ያልተፈለገ እርግዝና በመከላከል በቤተሰብ እቅድ፣ ከእርግዝና በፊት እና በእርግዝና ወቅት ጥሩ ጤንነት እና አመጋገብ፣ ጥራት ያለው እና አክብሮት ያለው የቅድመ ወሊድ እና የወሊድ እንክብካቤ እንዲሁም አዋላጆችን ጨምሮ በቂ የሰለጠነ የጤና ባለሙያዎችን ጨምሮ በቤተሰብ እቅድ አማካይነት የሟች ልደትን መከላከል ይቻላል። በእርግዝና ወቅት የቂጥኝ ሕክምና 200,000 የሚገመቱ የሟች ልደቶችን ሊከላከል ይችላል፣ የጽንስ የልብ ምት ክትትል እና የወሊድ ክትትል እና አስፈላጊ በሚሆንበት ጊዜ ፈጣን ጣልቃ ገብነት 832,000 የሚሆኑ በፅንስ ውስጥ የሟች ልደቶችን ለመከላከል እና የአራስ ሕፃናት ሞት ለመቀነስ ወሳኝ ናቸው። የሟች ሕፃናት መወለድ ከአሉታዊ ማህበራዊና ኢኮኖሚያዊ የጤና ሁኔታዎች ጋር በጥብቅ የተቆራኘ ነው። መከላከያ እና ምላሽ ሰጭ እንክብካቤ በእናቶች ጤና እንክብካቤ ቀጣይነት እና ከዚያ ባሻገር ማዋሃድ ያስፈልጋል። ይህ ደግሞ ሞት በሚከሰትበት ጊዜ አክብሮት የተሞላበትና ድጋፍ ሰጪ እንክብካቤ ማድረግን ይጨምራል። የሴቶችንና የማህበረሰቦቻቸውን ተሞክሮና ድምፅ ማዳመጥ ከሟች ሕፃናት ጋር ተያይዞ የሚመጣውን የመገለል ችግር ለመቅረፍ ወሳኝ ነው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፦ ለሴቶች፣ ለታዳጊዎች እና ለሕፃናት ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው የጤና እንክብካቤ ለማቅረብ የሟች ልደት መከላከልን በዓለም አቀፍ እና በብሔራዊ አጀንዳዎች ውስጥ ለማካተት የበለጠ መደረግ አለበት። የሴቶች፣ የሕፃናት እና ታዳጊዎች ጤና ዓለም አቀፍ ስትራቴጂ እ.ኤ.አ. 2016 - 2030 እና የእያንዳንዱ አዲስ የተወለደ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር( 2014) ምክሮችን በመተግበር የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የእናቶችን፣ አዲስ የተወለዱትን፣ የሕፃናትን እና ታዳጊዎችን ጤና ለማሻሻል በሚያደርገው ጥረት አካል ሆኖ ሊከላከሉ የሚችሉ የሞተ ልደቶችን ለማስቆም እየሰራ ነው። ብዙዎቹ የሞቱ ሕፃናት የልደት የምስክር ወረቀት አይቀበሉም እና አልተመዘገቡም። የወሊድ እና የአራስ ሞትን ሪፖርት የማድረግ ሥርዓቶችን ማሻሻል የሰብዓዊ መብቶች ጒዳይ ሲሆን የሟች ልደትን ለመቀነስ ቅድመ ሁኔታ ነው። በተጨማሪም የሞቱ ሕፃናትን በትክክል ለመያዝ እና ሪፖርት ለማድረግ መደበኛ የጤና መረጃ ስርዓቶችን ማጠናከር እና የእንክብካቤ ጥራትን ማሻሻል ያስፈልጋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አገራት የሟች ሕፃናት እና የሕፃናት ሞት መረጃዎቻቸውን እንዲያሻሽሉ የሚያግዙ መሣሪያዎችን አቅርቧል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሀገሮች የግለሰቦችን ሞት እንዲገመግሙ እና እንዲመረመሩ የሚረዳ መመሪያ አለው ስለዚህ ለወደፊቱ ተመሳሳይ ሁኔታዎችን ለመከላከል መፍትሄዎችን እንዲመክሩ እና እንዲተገብሩ እና ለቅድመ ወሊድ ሞት (አይሲዲ-ፒኤም) መደበኛ ምደባ ስርዓት። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና ዩኒሴፍ ሊከላከሉ የሚችሉ የሞተ ልደቶችን ለማስቆም የጋራ እርምጃ እንዲወሰድ ጥሪን እየደገፉ ነው።
translations.reference: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics. Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond. This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered. Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.hypothesis: Stillbirth: Stillbirth refers to the death of a fetus after 28 weeks of gestation, but before or during delivery. It is a significant public health problem, with an estimated 2 million stillbirths occurring worldwide each year. The majority of stillbirths, approximately 40%, occur during the third trimester. This loss can be prevented through improved quality of care and access to emergency obstetric services. Stillbirth is a global issue that receives insufficient attention in international agendas, policies, and evidence-based programs. It disproportionately affects women and their families, leading to significant emotional, social, and economic consequences. For example, women who experience a stillbirth may suffer from post-traumatic stress disorder, depression, and financial hardship. In 2014, the World Health Organization (WHO) adopted a new global strategy to reduce maternal mortality and stillbirths by 2030. The strategy calls for each country to implement a comprehensive program of evidence-based interventions, including improved antenatal care, skilled birth attendance, and emergency obstetric care. By 2021, 139 countries had made progress towards achieving this goal. However, without further efforts, an estimated 56 countries will not meet the target by 2030. If the current trend continues, an estimated 15.9 million children will die from stillbirth. Half of these deaths, or 7.7 million, are expected to occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: The majority of stillbirths are preventable with quality healthcare during pregnancy and childbirth. The main causes of stillbirth include pregnancy-related complications, such as pre-eclampsia, eclampsia, and postpartum hemorrhage; prolonged pregnancy; and infections, such as syphilis, HIV, and urinary tract infections. In high-income countries, a significant proportion of stillbirths are attributed to preterm birth, which is often caused by complications such as pre-eclampsia and multiple pregnancies. The exact number of stillbirths in low-income countries is unknown due to limited data. Risk factors: Several factors can increase the risk of stillbirth, including maternal age, parity (number of previous pregnancies), and access to quality healthcare. Unintended pregnancies are also associated with an increased risk of stillbirth. Prevention: Stillbirth can be prevented through a combination of interventions, including: improved antenatal care, skilled birth attendance, and access to emergency obstetric services; prevention and treatment of infections; and addressing social and economic factors that contribute to poor maternal health. Antenatal care, including screening for pre-eclampsia and other pregnancy complications, can help to identify and manage risk factors before they lead to stillbirth. Skilled birth attendance, which includes a trained healthcare provider present during delivery, can help to prevent complications such as postpartum hemorrhage and fetal distress. Emergency obstetric services, such as access to blood transfusions and cesarean sections, can help to save the lives of both mothers and babies.

translations.source: ግንዛቤን መጨመር እና መገለልን መቀነስ። የተጐዱ ሴቶችን እና ቤተሰቦችን መደገፍ። ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው እንክብካቤ ለመስጠት የጤና ሥርዓቶችን ማጠናከር። የሞተ ልደት ዒላማዎችን ብሔራዊ እና አካባቢያዊ ማድረግ። ማስረጃዎችን እና እውቀትን ለማሳደግ የሞተ ልደት መለኪያዎችን ማሻሻል።
translations.reference: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.hypothesis: Enhance awareness and reduce stigma. Support affected women and families. Strengthen healthcare systems to provide high-quality care. Make maternal mortality targets national and local. Improve maternal mortality indicators to increase evidence and knowledge.

translations.source: ዝቅተኛ ጥራት ያላቸው እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ በአለም ላይ እስከ ሁለት ቢሊዮን የሚደርሱ ሰዎች አስፈላጊ የሆኑ መድሃኒቶችን፣ ክትባቶችን፣ በብልቃጥ ምርመራን ጨምሮ የህክምና መሳሪያዎች እና ሌሎች የጤና ምርቶችን አያገኙም። ይህም ብዙውን ጊዜ ጥራት በሌላቸው እና በተጭበረበሩ ምርቶች የተሞላ ክፍተት ይፈጥራል። ይህ ችግር እየጨመረ የመጣው ዓለም አቀፋዊ የአቅርቦት ሰንሰለቶች ይበልጥ ውስብስብ እየሆኑ በመምጣታቸው በአንድ ሀገር ውስጥ የተመረቱ ምርቶች በሁለተኛ ሀገር ውስጥ የታሸጉ እና በሦስተኛ ሀገር ውስጥ ለሸማቾች ለመሸጥ ወይም ለመሸጥ ድንበር ተሻጋሪ ሊሆኑ ይችላሉ። የኤሌክትሮኒክ ንግድ እድገት ለዚህ አዝማሚያ አስተዋጽኦ የሚያበረክተው ብዙውን ጊዜ ከማይፈቀዱ ምንጮች በመስመር ላይ መድኃኒቶችን መግዛትን ቀላል በማድረግ ነው። በዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሃገራት ከአሥር መድኃኒቶች ውስጥ አንዱ ዝቅተኛ ጥራት ያለው ወይም የተጭበረበረ መሆኑን ከግምት በማስገባት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ይህንን ጒዳይ ለሚቀጥሉት አስርት ዓመታት አስቸኳይ የጤና ችግሮች አንዱ አድርጎታል። በዚህ ጒዳይ ያልተነካ ሀገር የለም። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከሁሉም የዓለም ክልሎች ጥራት የሌላቸው ወይም የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና መድኃኒቶች፣ ክትባቶች እና በብልቃጥ ውስጥ ምርመራዎች ሪፖርቶችን ተቀብሏል። ለካንሰር ከሚያገለግሉ በጣም ውድ ምርቶች ጀምሮ እስከ ሕመም ማስታገሻ በጣም ርካሽ ምርቶች ድረስ ሁለቱም አጠቃላይ እና ፈጠራ መድኃኒቶች ሊታለሉ ይችላሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል አገራት ጥራት ያላቸውን፣ ደህንነታቸው የተጠበቀ፣ ውጤታማ እና ጥራት ያላቸውን የህክምና ምርቶች ተደራሽነት ለማሳደግ በመከላከል፣ በመለየት እና ምላሽ ላይ ያተኰረ አጠቃላይ ዓለም አቀፍ ስትራቴጂ ላይ ተስማምተዋል። ተጽዕኖ: ጥራት የሌላቸውና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች መድኃኒቱን ለሚወስዱ ግለሰቦችም ሆኑ ለቤተሰቦች፣ ለትልቁ ኅብረተሰብ፣ ለጤና እንክብካቤ ሥርዓት እና ለአቅርቦት ሰንሰለት ብዙ ችግሮችን ይፈጥራሉ። መድኃኒቶች በተገቢው መንገድ የማይሰሩ ከሆነ በሽታን ማከም ወይም መከላከል አይችሉም። ለፀረ-ተህዋሲያን መድኃኒቶች የመቋቋም አቅም እንዲኖር አስተዋጽኦ ያደርጋሉ። ውድ ሀብቶችን ያባክናሉ። እንዲሁም በመድኃኒቶች እና በጤና አጠባበቅ አቅራቢዎች ላይ እምነት ማጣት ያስከትላሉ። ይህ አሉታዊ ማህበረ-ኢኮኖሚያዊ ተፅእኖን ሊያስከትል ይችላል። ለምርታማነት መጥፋት እና ለግለሰብ እና ለሀገር አቀፍ የጤና ስርዓት ወጪዎችን ይጨምራል። ጥራት ያለው እና ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ የህክምና ምርቶች አቅርቦት ውስንነት፣ የአስተዳደር ጉድለት እና የቴክኒክ አቅም ደካማ በሆነባቸው ሁኔታዎች ውስጥ ታካሚዎችን የመድረስ እድላቸው ሰፊ ነው። ምንም እንኳን ይህ ጉዳይ ሁሉንም ሀገራት የሚመለከት ቢሆንም ደካማ ወይም የቁጥጥር ስርዓቶች የለሉባቸው ሀገራት ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች ትልቁን ሸክም ይሸከማሉ። ከጊዜ ወደ ጊዜ እየጨመረ በሚሄደው ግሎባላይዜሽን ዓለም ውስጥ አንድም ሀገር ይህንን ጒዳይ በተናጠል ለመቋቋም የሚያስችል በቂ ሀብትና አቅም የለውም። እንደ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዓለም አቀፍ ክትትል እና ቁጥጥር ስርዓት እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገር ሜካኒዝም ባሉ ዓለም አቀፍ ስርዓቶች እና ሂደቶች አባል ሀገራት ከደረጃ በታች የሆኑ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶችን ለመከላከል፣ ለመለየት እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት በጋራ እየሰሩ ነው።
translations.reference: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified. No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers. This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism, Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: A comprehensive overview of substandard and counterfeit medical products: An estimated two billion people worldwide do not have access to essential medicines, vaccines, diagnostic products, and other health products. This often results in a shortage of high-quality, safe products. This problem is exacerbated by the increasing complexity of global supply chains, which can make it difficult to track the origin of products and ensure their authenticity. The rise of e-commerce has also made it easier to purchase counterfeit drugs online, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. The World Health Organization (WHO) has identified the problem of substandard and counterfeit medical products as one of the most pressing health challenges of the 21st century. There is no country that is immune to this problem. The WHO has received reports of substandard and counterfeit medicines, vaccines, and diagnostic products from all regions of the world. These products can range from expensive cancer drugs to very cheap pain relievers. The WHO is working with its member states to develop a comprehensive global strategy to combat substandard and counterfeit medical products. This strategy includes measures to prevent, detect, and respond to these products. The impact of substandard and counterfeit medical products can be devastating. These products can be ineffective or even harmful, leading to serious health consequences or even death. They can also undermine public trust in the healthcare system and in the pharmaceutical industry. This can have a negative impact on public health and on the economy.

translations.source: ራስን ማጥፋትን መከላከል አጠቃላይ እይታ:- በየዓመቱ ከ700,000 በላይ ሰዎች ራሳቸውን ያጠፋሉ። ለእያንዳንዱ ራስን ማጥፋት ከ20 በላይ ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራዎች እንዳሉ ይገመታል። ራስን ማጥፋት በማንኛውም የሕይወት ደረጃ እና በሁሉም የዓለም ክፍሎች ሊከሰት ይችላል። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2019 ራስን ማጥፋት በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከ15-29 አመት እድሜ ክልል ውስጥ ለሚከሰቱ አራተኛው ሞት ምክንያት ሲሆን 77በመቶ የሚሆኑት ራስን የማጥፋት ድርጊቶች የተከሰቱት ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ነው። በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ 20% የሚሆኑት ራሳቸውን የሚያጠፉ ሰዎች ራሳቸውን በፀረ ተባይ መድኃኒቶች በመመረዝ ነው። ራስን ማጥፋት እና የአዕምሮ ጤና ሁኔታዎች( በተለይም የመንፈስ ጭንቀት እና የአልኮል አጠቃቀም ችግሮች) እና ቀደም ሲል ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራ በከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገሮች ውስጥ በደንብ የተረጋገጠ ቢሆንም ብዙ ራስን የማጥፋት ድርጊቶች በችግር ጊዜያት በድንገት ይከሰታሉ። ራስን የማጥፋት አደጋ የሚፈጥሩ ሌሎች ምክንያቶች ደግሞ የኑሮ ውድነት፣ ብቸኝነት፣ መድልዎ፣ የግንኙነት መቋረጥ፣ የገንዘብ ችግር፣ ሥር የሰደደ ህመምና ሕመም፣ ጥቃት፣ በደል፣ ግጭት ወይም ሌሎች የሰብዓዊ አደጋዎች ናቸው። በአእምሮ ጤንነት ችግሮች እና ራስን በማጥፋት ዙሪያ ያለው ቅሌት (መገለል) ብዙ ሰዎች ራስን የማጥፋት ችግር እርዳታ አይፈልግም ብለው ማሰባቸው ነው። ራስን ማጥፋት እና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራዎች በቤተሰብ፣ በጓደኞች፣ በስራ ባልደረቦች፣ በማህበረሰቦች እና ማህበራት ላይ የሚያሳድሩት ተፅዕኖዎች አሉት። ራስን ማጥፋት: በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ እውነታዎች እና ቍጥሮች። እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 በዓለም ዙሪያ ራስን ማጥፋት ሪፖርት ህይወትን መኖር: በአገሮች ውስጥ ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል የአተገባበር መመሪያ። መከላከል: ራስን ማጥፋትንና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራን ለመከላከል በግለሰብ፣ በማህበረሰብና በብሔራዊ ደረጃ ብዙ ነገር ማድረግ ይቻላል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የህይወትን መኖር አቀራረብ በአራት ውጤታማ፣ በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ቍልፍ ጣልቃ ገብነቶች ይመክራል። እነዚህም ራስን ለማጥፋት በሚደረገው እያንዳንዱ ብሔራዊ ምላሽ ውስጥ መካተት አለባቸው። ራስን ወደ ማጥፋት የሚያመሩ ነገሮችን መገደብ። ራስን ማጥፋትን በኃላፊነት ሪፖርት ለማድረግ ከመገናኛ ብዙኃን ጋር መስተጋብር መፍጠር። በጉርምስና (በአስራዎቹ) ዕድሜ ላይ ባሉ ወጣቶች ላይ ማህበራዊ-ስሜታዊ የህይወት ክህሎቶችን ማዳበር። ራስን የማጥፋት ዝንባሌ ያላቸውን ሰዎች በቅድሚያ መለየት፣ መገምገም፣ ማስተዳደር እና ክትትል ማድረግ። የእርምጃዎቹ መሰረታዊ ምሰሶዎች የሁኔታ ትንተና፣ ዘርፈ ብዙ ትብብር፣ የግንዛቤ ማስጨበጫ፣ የአቅም ግንባታ፣ ፋይናንስ፣ ቁጥጥር፣ ክትትል እና ግምገማ ናቸው። ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል የሚደረገው ጥረት በጤና፣ በትምህርት፣ በሰራተኛ፣ በግብርና፣ በንግድ፣ በፍትህ፣ በሕግ፣ በመከላከያ፣ በፖለቲካና በመገናኛ ብዙኃን ዘርፎች መካከል ቅንጅትና ትብብርን ይጠይቃል። ራስን ማጥፋት ዋነኛው የህብረተሰብ ጤና ችግር መሆኑን በመገለል እና በግንዛቤ ማነስ ምክንያት ራስን ማጥፋትን መከላከል በቂ ምላሽ አይሰጥም። ራስን ማጥፋትን በመከላከል ረገድ ማኅበረሰቦችም ወሳኝ ሚና ይጫወታሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ መሳሪያ ስብስብ ማህበረሰቦችን ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ነገሮች በመለየት እና ተግባራትን እንደየአካባቢው ሁኔታ እንዲተገብሩ መመሪያ ይሰጣል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፦ ራስን የማጥፋት ድርጊቶችን ለመከላከል አስቸኳይ እርምጃ መውሰድ እንዳለበት በከፍተኛ ደረጃ ተገንዝቦ ቅድሚያ ተሰጥቶታል።
translations.reference: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries. Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies. Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation. Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels.
translations.hypothesis: Suicide is a major public health issue worldwide. In 2019, suicide was the 15th-29 leading cause of death globally, accounting for 1.4% of all deaths. Suicide rates vary significantly across countries, with the highest rates in Europe and the Americas. Suicide is often associated with mental health conditions, such as depression, anxiety, and substance abuse. However, suicide can also occur in individuals without a diagnosed mental health condition. Factors that increase the risk of suicide include a history of suicide attempts, a family history of suicide, and exposure to violence or trauma. Suicide is a complex issue with no single cause. It is important to recognize the warning signs of suicide and to seek help for those who are at risk. Suicide prevention efforts should focus on reducing access to lethal means, promoting mental health awareness, and providing support to those who are struggling.

translations.source: በአገሮች ውስጥ ራስን የማጥፋት መጠን መቀነስ በተባበሩት መንግሥታት ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች( ለአእምሮ ጤና ብቸኛው አመላካች) በዓለም የጤና ድርጅት አጠቃላይ የሥራ ፕሮግራም እና በዓለም የጤና ድርጅት አጠቃላይ የአዕምሮ ጤና የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር 2013 - 2030 ውስጥ አመላካች ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2014 የታተመው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመጀመሪያ የዓለም ራስን ማጥፋት ሪፖርት ራስን ማጥፋትን መከላከል ዓለም አቀፋዊ ግዴታ ነው። ራስን የማጥፋት እና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራዎች የሕዝብ ጤና ጠቀሜታ ግንዛቤን ለማሳደግ እና ራስን የማጥፋት መከላከያ የሕዝብ ጤና ቅድሚያ እንዲሰጥ ለማድረግ ነበር.። መንግሥታት ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል ውጤታማ እና በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ቍልፍ ጣልቃ ገብነቶች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የ ህይወትን መኖር ጥቅል ተግባራዊ እንዲያደርጉ እና በበርካታ ዘርፎች የሕዝብ ጤና አቀራረብ ውስጥ ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል አጠቃላይ ብሔራዊ ስትራቴጂዎችን እንዲያዘጋጁ ወይም እንዲያጠናክሩ ይበረታታሉ። ራስን ማጥፋትን ለመከላከል ብሔራዊ አመራር እና ቅንጅት በብሔራዊ ራስን ማጥፋት መከላከል ስትራቴጂዎች-እድገቶች ፣ ምሳሌዎች እና አመላካቾች በማተም የበለጠ ይደገፋሉ ። መረጃ እና ስታቲስቲክስ ለጥብቅና እና ውሳኔ አሰጣጥ አስፈላጊ መሆናቸውን በመገንዘብ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 ራስን ማጥፋትን በዓለም ዙሪያ አሳተመ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ራእይ ራስን በማጥፋት ሞት ከአሁን በኋላ ዋነኛው የሞት መንስኤ የማይሆንበት እና ራስን የማጥፋት ሙከራ እያደረጉ ወይም እያሰቡ ያሉ ሰዎች ለሁሉም ብቃት ባለው እና በአክብሮት የሚሰጥ እርዳታ በመፈለግ ምቾት የሚሰማቸው የሚሆኑበትን ዓለም ማየት ነው።
translations.reference: The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates. WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.
translations.hypothesis: Reducing suicide rates in countries is a key indicator in the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals (specifically, the only mental health indicator), as outlined in the World Health Organization's (WHO) Global Mental Health Action Plan 2013-2030. The WHO's first global suicide report, published in 2014, emphasizes suicide prevention as a global responsibility. The aim of suicide prevention efforts is to raise awareness of the impact of suicide and to prioritize suicide prevention within public health. Governments are encouraged by the WHO to implement effective and evidence-based interventions to prevent suicide, and to develop or strengthen national strategies for suicide prevention as part of a broader public health approach. National leadership and coordination are crucial for successful suicide prevention efforts, and the WHO provides guidance and support in this area. Recognizing the importance of data and statistics for informed decision-making, the WHO has also highlighted suicide prevention as a priority. The WHO's vision is a world where suicide is no longer the leading cause of death, and where people who are contemplating or attempting suicide can access timely and appropriate support.

translations.source: ዘላቂ ልማት(ቀጣይነት ያለው እድገት) አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ ዘላቂ ልማት ማለት ለወደፊት የአካባቢን፣ ማህበራዊ እና የግል ጤናን ሳይከፍሉ ዛሬ ጥቅማጥቅሞችን የሚሰጡ ፖሊሲዎችን፣ ፕሮጀክቶችን እና ኢንቨስትመንቶችን ለመግለጽ ሰፊ ቃል ነው። እነዚህ ፖሊሲዎች ብዙውን ጊዜ አረንጓዴ ተብለው ይጠራሉ። ምክንያቱም የልማትን በአካባቢ ላይ የሚያሳድረውን ተጽዕኖ ለመገደብ ያተኮሩ ናቸው። ሆኖም የዘላቂ ልማት ጥቅሞች በሰፊ የሰው ልጅ ጤና እና ደህንነት ላይም ይሰማሉ። ይህም የብክለት እና የአካባቢ-ነክ በሽታዎችን መቀነስ፣ የተሻሻሉ የጤና ውጤቶችን እና የጭንቀት መቀነስን ይጨምራል። የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ስጋቶች ከጊዜ ወደ ጊዜ እየጨመሩ በመምጣታቸው የአየር ንብረት ለውጥን ለመቀነስ፣ ለጤና መሻሻል እና ለወጪ ቁጠባ ሁሉን ተጠቃሚ የሚያደርጉ ስትራቴጂዎች ለተለያዩ ባለድርሻ አካላት በርካታ ጥቅሞችን ይሰጣሉ። ለምሳሌ አረንጓዴ የጤና እንክብካቤ ክዋኔዎች ለታካሚዎች እና ለሰራተኞች የጤና ጥቅሞችን ሊያመጡ ይችላሉ። እንዲሁም ኃይል ይቆጥባሉ፣ የአየር ንብረት አደጋዎችን ይቀንሳሉ እና የረጅም ጊዜ ወጪ ቁጠባን ይፈጥራሉ። የዓለም የጤና ድርጅት የወደፊቱን ሕዝብ ከዛሬው እየጨመረ ከሚመጣ የጤና አደጋ ለመጠበቅ በሚያደርገው ሥራ ሁሉ ዘላቂ ልማት ለማሳካት ቁርጠኛ ነው። ተጽዕኖ: ብዙ ዘላቂ የልማት ስትራቴጂዎች ለጤና፣ ለአየር ንብረት እና ለአካባቢ ከፍተኛ ትርፍ ሊያገኙ ይችላሉ። እናም ጥቅሞቹ ወዲያውኑ ሊታዩ ይችላሉ። ለምሳሌ በዓመት ወደ 7 ሚሊዮን የሚገመቱ ያለዕድሜ ሞት ምክንያት የሆነውን የአየር ብክለትን በመታገል ብዙ የጤና እና የልማት ግቦችን በአንድ ጊዜ ማሳካት ይቻላል። የብክለት ልቀቶች ኃይለኛ የአጭር ጊዜ የአየር ንብረት በካይ (ኤስኤልሲፒዎች) -በተለይ ጥቁር ካርቦን፣ ኦዞን እና ሚቴን ለጤና በጣም ጎጂ የሆኑ ነገር ግን አጭር የከባቢ አየር ህይወት ያላቸው ናቸው። የንጹሕ የሕዝብ ትራንስፖርትን የሚደግፉ የትራንስፖርት ፖሊሲዎች እና ኢንቨስትመንቶች በእግር እና በብስክሌት መጓዝ በአየር ጥራት ላይ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ ሊያሳድሩ ይችላሉ። ይህ የደም ቧንቧን፣የልብ ድካም፣ የሳንባ በሽታ እና አንዳንድ የካንሰር በሽታዎችን ጨምሮ በርካታ የጤና አደጋዎችን በአንድ ጊዜ ሊቀንስ ይችላል። በተጨማሪም በየዓመቱ ከትራፊክ ጕዳት የሚሞቱትን 1,25 ሚሊዮን የሚገመቱ ሰዎችን ለመቀነስ እና የጤና አገልግሎቶችን በተለይም በሰፊው በሚኖሩ አካባቢዎች ተደራሽነትን ለማሻሻል ይችላሉ። ዘላቂ ልማት የቤቶች ፖሊሲዎች እንደ የሕንጻ ቦታ እና የመሬት አጠቃቀም፣ የግንባታ ቁሳቁሶች ምርጫ፣ የዲዛይን ባህሪዎች እና የአየር ማናፈሻ እና የኃይል አቅርቦት ባሉ ጒዳዮች አማካይነት ልቀቶችን የበለጠ ሊቀንስ ይችላል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሥራ የሚመራው በዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች በተለይም በግብ 3 ነው። ጤናማ ኑሮዎችን ማረጋገጥ እና በሁሉም የዕድሜ ክልል ውስጥ ላሉት ሁሉ ደኅንነትን ማጐልበት። ግቦቹ በመላ ድርጅቱ ፕሮጀክቶች እና ፖሊሲዎች ላይ የመኖሪያ ቤት፣ የከተማ ልማት፣ የትራንስፖርት ሥርዓቶች እና የጤና እንክብካቤ አቅርቦትን ጨምሮ መመሪያዎችን ይሰጣሉ። ለምሳሌ: በ 2018 ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ የታተሙት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመኖሪያ ቤት እና የጤና መመሪያዎች የከተማ እድገት፣ የአየር ንብረት እና የስነህዝብ ለውጦች አንጻር የመኖሪያ ቤት ሁኔታዎች በሰዎች ጤና ላይ የሚያሳድሩትን ተጽዕኖ ያሳያሉ። መመሪያዎቹ ለአባል አገራት፣ ለአጋሮች እና ለሕዝብ በቂ ያልሆነ የመኖሪያ ቦታ( የሕዝብ ብዛት)፣ ዝቅተኛ እና ከፍተኛ የቤት ውስጥ ሙቀቶች፣ በቤት ውስጥ የመጉዳት አደጋዎች እና የአካል ጉዳተኞች የመኖሪያ ቤት ተደራሽነት ጋር የሚዛመዱ በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ምክሮችን ይሰጣሉ። የከተማ ጤና ኢኒሼቲቭ (ዩኤችአይ) በከተማ የአየር ብክለት እና በአጭር ጊዜ የሚቆዩ የአየር ንብረት ብክለትን (ኤስኤልሲፒስ) ላይ ውጤታማ እርምጃ በመውሰድ ጤናን፣ አካባቢን እና ዘላቂ ልማትን በማስተሳሰር ህይወትን በመታደግ ጤናን በሚያስችሉ መንገዶች ላይ ያተኩራል። በተጨማሪም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የአየር ብክለትን ለመቀነስ እና በየዓመቱ የሚያደርሰውን 7 ሚሊዮን ያለጊዜው የሚሞቱትን ሞት በመቀነስ በሕይወት መተንፈስ ዘመቻ ላይ ከአየር ንብረት እና ንፁህ አየር ጥምረት (ሲሲኤሲ) ጋር ይተባበራል።
translations.reference: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders. For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes. Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.” The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.
translations.hypothesis: Sustainable Development: A Comprehensive Overview: Sustainable development refers to a broad concept that encompasses policies, projects, and investments aimed at providing benefits today without compromising the well-being of future generations, including the environment, society, and individuals. These policies often focus on environmental sustainability, as they aim to minimize the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development also extend to human health and well-being. This includes reducing pollution and environmental-related diseases, improving health outcomes, and reducing stress. As concerns about climate change continue to grow, strategies that mitigate climate change, improve health, and promote cost savings are becoming increasingly important for a wide range of stakeholders. For example, green healthcare initiatives can provide health benefits for patients and staff, while also reducing costs and improving environmental performance. Additionally, they can help to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve air quality, benefiting the entire community. The World Health Organization (WHO) is committed to achieving sustainable development in order to protect the health of the current and future population. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can have a significant impact on health, climate, and the environment. And these benefits can be realized quickly. For example, by addressing air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million deaths per year, we can achieve multiple health and development goals at the same time. Air pollutants, such as particulate matter (PM), ozone, and nitrogen dioxide, are harmful to human health, but they also have a significant impact on the environment. Policies and investments that promote clean transportation, such as walking and cycling, can have a significant impact on air quality. This can help to reduce a wide range of health problems, including respiratory diseases, heart disease, stroke, and certain types of cancer. Additionally, it can help to reduce traffic fatalities, which claim the lives of 1.25 million people each year. Sustainable development policies can also have a positive impact on housing. By addressing issues such as building location and land use, building materials, design features, and ventilation and energy efficiency, we can reduce the environmental impact of housing. World Health Organization (WHO) Response: The World Health Organization (WHO) is working to achieve sustainable development through its Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly Goal 3: Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at every age. These goals provide a framework for the WHO's work on a wide range of issues, including health, housing, urban development, transportation, and health care. For example, the WHO's Global Guidelines on Physical Activity recommend that people of all ages engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity or 75 minutes of vigorous-intensity physical activity per week. These guidelines are based on scientific evidence and provide practical advice for policymakers, healthcare professionals, and the public. The WHO is also working to improve access to adequate housing, which is essential for health and well-being. translations.source: ቂጥኝ አጠቃላይ እይታ: ሲፊሊስ በ ትሬፖኔማ ፓሊደም ምክንያት የሚመጣ በባክቴሪያ የሚተላለፍ በግብረ ሥጋ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፍ ኢንፌክሽን( ኤስ.ቲ.አይ) ነው። ይህም ከፍተኛ ሕመም እና ሞት ያስከትላል። እና ሊድን ይችላል። ቂጥኝ የሚተላለፈው በወሲባዊ ግንኙነት፣ በደም ዝውውር ወይም ከነፍሰ ጡር ሴት ወደ ፅንሱ ነው። ከእናት ወደ ልጅ የሚተላለፈው የቂጥኝ በሽታ በእርግዝና መጀመሪያ ላይ ካልተገኘ ወይም በበቂ ሁኔታ ካልታከመ በፅንስ ላይ ከፍተኛ ጕዳት ያስከትላል። የእናቶች ቂጥኝ ህክምና ካልተደረገለት፣ ዘግይቶ ህክምና ከተደረገለት ወይም ፔኒሲሊን ካልተደረገለት በቂጥኝ ደረጃ ላይ በመመርኮዝ ከ50−80% በሚሆኑ ጒዳዮች ላይ አሉታዊ የወሊድ ውጤቶች( ኤቢኦ) ያስከትላል። እነዚህ ኤቢኦስ ብዙ ጊዜ ከባድ ናቸው። እና በሞት መወለድ (በተለምዶ)፣ በአራስ ሕፃን ሞት፣ ያለጊዜው መወለድ፣ ዝቅተኛ ክብደት እና በወሊድ የተያዙ ሕፃናትን ያጠቃልላሉ። በትክክል እና ተከታታይ ኮንዶም መጠቀምን ጨምሮ ደህንነቱ በተጠበቀ የግብረ ሥጋ ግንኙነት ቂጥኝን መከላከል ይቻላል። ብዙ የቂጥኝ በሽታ ያለባቸው ሰዎች ምንም ምልክት አይታይባቸውም ወይም በቀላሉ የማይታዩ ጥቃቅን ምልክቶች አሏቸው። በላብራቶሪ ምርመራዎች አማካኝነት ኢንፌክሽኑን መለየት እና አዎንታዊ ጒዳዮችን ማከም ተጨማሪ ስርጭትን እና የተወለደ ቂጥኝ ጨምሮ አሉታዊ የእርግዝና ውጤቶችን ይከላከላል። ምርመራው በፈጣን ምርመራዎች ሊከናወን ይችላል። ይህም ውጤቱን ከ 20 ደቂቃ ባነሰ ጊዜ ውስጥ ሊሰጥ ይችላል። ይህም ፈጣን ሕክምናን ይፈቅዳል። ካልታከመ ቂጥኝ ለብዙ ዓመታት የሚቆይ ሲሆን በተለያዩ ደረጃዎች ውስጥ ያልፋል። የቂጥኝ ሕክምና በፔኒሲሊን ቤንዛታይን በመርፌ የሚሰጥ ሲሆን እንደ በሽታው ደረጃ ከ 1 እስከ 3 መጠን ይወሰዳል። የትውልድ ቂጥኝን መከላከል የሚችለው ፔኒሲሊን ብቻ ነው። ከፍተኛ፣ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸውን አገሮችን ጨምሮ በበርካታ አገሮች ውስጥ የቤንዛቲን ፔኒሲሊን በየጊዜው እና ረዘም ላለ ጊዜ እጥረት በመኖሩ የፔኒሲሊን ህክምና ተግዳሮት ሆኗል። የበሽታው ምልክቶች፦ በቂጥኝ የተያዙ ብዙ ሰዎች የበሽታው ምልክቶች አይታዩባቸውም ወይም እንደታዩባቸው አይገነዘቡትም። ህክምና ካልተደረገለት ቂጥኝ ለበርካታ ዓመታት የሚቆይ ሲሆን በተለያዩ ደረጃዎች ይገለጻል። ወሲባዊ ስርጭት በአብዛኛው የሚከሰተው በቅድሚያ ደረጃዎች ማለትም ከበሽታው በኋላ እስከ 2 ዓመት ድረስ ነው። በአማካይ 21 ቀናት በሚቆየው የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ( የመጀመሪያው ሲፊሊስ) ወቅት በክትባት ቦታ( በግንኙነት) ላይ ብቸኛ፣ ሕመም የሌለው፣ ብዙውን ጊዜ ከባድ እና ክብ የሆነ ቍስል( ቻንከር) ይታያል። ብዙ ጊዜ በሴት ብልት፣ በወንድ ብልት ወይም ፊንጢጣ (ነገር ግን ከብልት ውጪ ሊሆን ይችላል) ይህ ሳይስተዋል አይቀርም። ቻንክሬው ከ3 እስከ 10 ሳምንታት ውስጥ በህክምናም ሆነ ያለ ህክምና የሚፈወስ ነው። ህክምና ካልተደረገለት በሽታው ወደ ሁለተኛ ደረጃ ይሸጋገራል። የሁለተኛ ደረጃ ቂጥኝ በሰፊው ሊለያይ በሚችል እና ሌሎች ተላላፊ ወይም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ ሁኔታዎችን በሚመስሉ ብጕር ተለይቶ ይታወቃል። ግን በተለምዶ መዳፎችን እና እግሮችን ይነካል። ሽፍታው የማያሳክክ እና ሊታለፍ የሚችል አነስተኛ ሊሆን ይችላል። እንደ ፊንጢጣ እና ከናፍረምስ ባሉ ሞቃታማ እና እርጥብ በሆኑ የሰውነት ክፍሎች ውስጥ ዋናው ቻንከር (ኮንዶሎማ ላታ) በሚገኝበት ቦታ ላይ ትልቅ ነጭ ወይም ግራጫ ከፍ ያሉ ቁስሎች ሊፈጠሩ ይችላሉ። የሁለተኛ ደረጃ ቂጥኝ ህመሞች እና ምልክቶች ያለ ህክምና እንኳን ሳይቀር በራስ-ሰር ይስተካከላሉ። ህክምና ካልተደረገለት የተደበቀው ደረጃ ይጀምራል።
translations.reference: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis. These ABOs are often severe and include stillbirth (most commonly), neonatal death, prematurity, low birth weight, and congenitally infected infants. Syphilis can be prevented through safer sex practices including the correct and consistent use of condoms. Many people with syphilis do not have any symptoms or have only minor symptoms that go unnoticed. Identifying infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes including congenital syphilis. Screening can be done using rapid tests that can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment. When untreated, syphilis lasts many years and goes through different stages. Treatment for syphilis is done with injectable penicillin benzathine, with 1 to 3 doses depending on the stage of the disease. Only penicillin can prevent congenital syphilis. Treatment with penicillin has been hampered by periodic and prolonged shortages of benzathine penicillin in numerous countries, including high-, low- and middle-income countries. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not present symptoms or do not perceive them. Untreated, syphilis lasts many years and is characterized into different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs during early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the initial phase (primary syphilis), which lasts on average 21 days, a solitary, painless, usually hard and round sore (chancre) appears at the site of inoculation (contact), often in the vagina, penis or anus (but it may also be extra-genital) and may go unnoticed. The chancre heals with or without treatment within 3 to 10 weeks. If untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage. Secondary syphilis is characterized by a rash that can vary widely and mimic other infectious or non-infectious conditions, but characteristically affects the palms and soles. The rash is non-itchy and can be minimal enough to be overlooked. In warm and moist areas of the body, such as the anus and labia, large white or grey raised lesions may develop at the location of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of secondary syphilis spontaneously resolve, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage starts.
translations.hypothesis:
Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by bacteria (Treponema pallidum). It can cause serious health problems and even death if left untreated. Syphilis is typically spread through sexual contact, but it can also be transmitted through blood or from a pregnant woman to her baby.

Syphilis can be prevented by practicing safe sex, avoiding sharing needles, and getting tested regularly. If you are pregnant and test positive for syphilis, it is important to get treatment immediately to prevent serious health problems for your baby.

Symptoms of syphilis vary depending on the stage of the infection. In the early stages, syphilis may cause a painless sore (chancre) on the genitals, rectum, or mouth. In later stages, syphilis can cause a variety of symptoms, including fever, fatigue, headache, rash, and nerve damage.

Syphilis is treated with antibiotics. The most common antibiotic used to treat syphilis is penicillin. The dosage of penicillin will vary depending on the stage of the infection.

If you think you may have syphilis, it is important to see a doctor as soon as possible. Syphilis is a treatable condition, but it is important to get treatment early to prevent serious health problems.

translations.source: የተደበቀ ሲፊሊስ ምንም ዓይነት ክሊኒካዊ ህመሞች ወይም ምልክቶች የሉትም። እናም ህክምናው ከመጀመሪያው ደረጃ የተለየ ነው። ለዓመታት ወይም ለአስርተ ዓመታት ህክምና ካልተደረገ የነርቭ፣ የልብና የደም ቧንቧ በሽታዎች ከሌሎች ጋር ሊከሰቱ ይችላሉ። ህክምና: የቂጥኝ ምርመራ የሚደረገው በሰውየው ታሪክ፣ በአካላዊ ምርመራ፣ በላቦራቶሪ ምርመራ እና አንዳንዴም በሬዲዮሎጂ ነው። ምክንያቱም ምልክቶቹ የተለመዱ ወይም የሚስተዋሉ አይደሉም። በላቦራቶሪ ምርመራዎች አማካኝነት ምልክት የሌለው ኢንፌክሽን መለየት እና አዎንታዊ ጒዳዮችን ማከም ተጨማሪ ስርጭትን እና የተወለደ ሲፊሊስን ጨምሮ አሉታዊ የእርግዝና ውጤቶችን ይከላከላል። ለቂጥኝ የሚደረጉ የላቦራቶሪ ምርመራዎች የቲ. ፓሊዲምን ቀጥተኛ ምርመራ ወይም እንደ ሴሮሎጂ ያሉ ቀጥተኛ ያልሆኑ ዘዴዎችን ያካትታሉ። ፈጣን ምርመራ እና የምርመራ ሙከራዎች እንዲሁ ይገኛሉ። እናም ውጤቱን ከ 20 ደቂቃ ባነሰ ጊዜ ውስጥ መስጠት ይችላሉ። ይህም ወዲያውኑ ህክምናን ለመጀመር ያመቻቻል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቂጥኝ በሽታው በደረጃው ላይ በመመርኮዝ በ 1 ወይም በ 3 መጠኖች በመርፌ በሚሰጥ ፔኒሲሊን( ቤንዛቲን ፔኒሲሊን ጂ) እንዲታከም ይመክራል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለሲፊሊስ ዝርዝር የህክምና መመሪያዎች አሉት። እና አንደኛው በተለይ በእርግዝና ወቅት ቂጥኝን ለመመርመር እና ለማከም። በዘር የሚተላለፍ ቂጥኝ በአሁኑ ጊዜ በዘር ለሚተላለፍ ቂጥኝ ምንም ዓይነት የምርመራ ሙከራዎች የሉም። ቂጥኝ ያለባቸው ሴቶች የሚወልዷቸው ሕፃናትም ሆኑ የሞቱ ሕፃናት በሙሉ በተወለዱበት ጊዜ በዘር የሚተላለፍ ቂጥኝ ምልክት አለመኖሩን መመርመር አለባቸው። በህይወት የተወለዱ ጨቅላ ህጻናት በተወለዱበት ጊዜ የሕክምና መስፈርቶችን ለመወሰን መመርመር እና መሞከር አለባቸው። በሚጠቁሙበት ጊዜ እንዲሁም በወርሃዊ ክፍተቶች ውስጥ ለ 3 ወራት የጨቅላ ሴሮሎጂ እስኪያልቅ ድረስ እና አሉታዊ ሆኖ እስኪቆይ፣ የእናቶች ፀረ-እንግዳ አካላት በእርግዝና ወቅት ወደ ሕፃኑ በቀላሉ ሊተላለፉ ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: Latent syphilis presents no clinical symptoms or signs and the treatment is different from the early phase. After years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular diseases among others may occur. Treatment: Syphilis diagnosis is based on the person’s history, physical examination, laboratory testing and sometimes radiology, as symptoms are not common or noticeable. Identifying asymptomatic infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology. Rapid screening and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in less than 20 minutes, facilitating immediate treatment initiation. WHO recommends syphilis to be treated with injectable penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) with 1 or 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. WHO has detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, and one specifically for the screening and treatment of syphilis in pregnancy. Congenital syphilis There are currently no diagnostic tests for congenital syphilis. All live or stillborn infants of women with syphilis should be examined for evidence of congenital syphilis. Live-born infants should be examined and tested at birth to define treatment requirement. When indicated, also at monthly intervals for 3 months until the infant’s serology are, and remain, negative, as maternal antibodies can be passively transmitted to the baby during pregnancy.
translations.hypothesis: Hidden syphilis does not present with any clinical symptoms or signs. Therefore, the treatment approach is different from that for other types of syphilis. If left untreated for years or even decades, it can lead to neurological, cardiovascular, and other health problems. Treatment: Diagnosis involves a combination of medical history, physical examination, laboratory tests, and sometimes, radiology. This is because the symptoms can be subtle or atypical. Laboratory tests can help detect asymptomatic infections and rule out false negatives, including pregnancy-related false negatives. Laboratory tests for syphilis may include direct detection methods, such as darkfield microscopy or serological tests. Rapid tests are also available, and results can be obtained in as little as 20 minutes. This allows for prompt treatment. The World Health Organization recommends treating syphilis with penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) in a single dose or three doses, depending on the severity of the infection. The World Health Organization also provides detailed guidelines for the treatment of syphilis. One important aspect of treatment is to screen pregnant women for syphilis and treat them if necessary. There are currently no specific tests for congenital syphilis. However, all infants born to mothers with syphilis should be screened for the presence of congenital syphilis at birth. Live-born infants should also be screened at birth to determine whether they are infected. In addition, pregnant women should be screened regularly for syphilis throughout their pregnancy, and treated if necessary.
